user_query:
I’m putting together a short, welcoming “Dark Fantasy Starter” set of reviews for my reading notes, and I want to integrate it with my simple site config and an index-generation script. Using the provided drafts and files in this workspace, please do all of the following and save your deliverables under the specified paths:

1) Rewrite the two draft reviews for tone and brevity:
- Input files: input/drafts/black_company.md and input/drafts/poppy_war.md.
- Keep each rewritten review between 120 and 180 words, spoiler-free, and in a tone that’s clear, concise, and evocative (welcoming to newcomers but still moody/dark).
- Preserve the existing YAML front matter fields exactly as-is (title, author, subgenre, rating, content_warnings, tags, source), and add one new field to the front matter: hook: "<one-sentence moody teaser>".
- Save the rewritten files to outputs/reviews/black_company.md and outputs/reviews/poppy_war.md, keeping the same filenames/stems.

2) Produce a structured summary for the reviews:
- Create outputs/reviews_index.json as a UTF-8 JSON array with one object per rewritten review.
- For each object, include exactly these fields with the given types:
  - title (string): from the front matter
  - author (string): from the front matter
  - subgenre (string): from the front matter
  - rating (integer): from the front matter as an integer
  - content_warnings (array of strings): from the front matter
  - hook (string): from the new front matter field you added
  - slug (string): the file stem (e.g., "black_company", "poppy_war")
  - path (string): relative path to the rewritten markdown file (e.g., "outputs/reviews/black_company.md")
  - word_count (integer): number of words in the rewritten body (count words by splitting on whitespace)
  - reading_time_minutes (integer): ceil(word_count / 200)
- Sort the array by rating descending, then title ascending before writing.

3) Update the simple site configuration so these new pages are discoverable:
- Modify site/site_config.yaml by adding a new top-level nav group named "Dark Fantasy" that lists the two rewritten review files. Each nav entry label should use the book title from the YAML front matter, and each path should point to the corresponding file under outputs/reviews/… (e.g., "outputs/reviews/black_company.md").

4) Adjust the index-generation script to match the new JSON location and expected schema:
- Modify scripts/generate_index.py to:
  - Set SOURCE_JSON = "outputs/reviews_index.json"
  - Set DEFAULT_OUTPUT = "outputs/index.md"
  - Set SORT_KEYS = ["-rating", "title"]  (interpret a leading "-" as descending for that key)
  - Update the module docstring to describe the expected JSON schema fields (title, author, subgenre, rating, content_warnings, hook, slug, path, word_count, reading_time_minutes) and the sort order (rating desc, then title asc). Do not implement new functionality; just update constants and documentation.

Notes and constraints:
- Do not introduce new external dependencies or require running any build.
- Keep the original front matter fields exactly the same values; only add the hook field.
- Ensure reviews_index.json is internally consistent with the rewritten markdown files (word_count and reading_time_minutes must be recomputable from the body text).
- Deliverables to produce/modify: outputs/reviews/black_company.md, outputs/reviews/poppy_war.md, outputs/reviews_index.json, site/site_config.yaml (modified), scripts/generate_index.py (modified). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
